title: Italian Lentil Soup (Zuppa di Lenticchie)

ingredients.name: brown or green lentils (masoor dal or any whole lentil)

subs.name: toor dal, moong dal or chana dal
subs.notes: toor dal gives a slightly softer, more soupy texture; all work well in this broth

subs.name: the green tops of spring onion or leek
subs.notes: spring onion tops give freshness; leek gives sweetness similar to celery

ingredients.name: ripe tomatoes, chopped, or canned tomatoes

subs.name: dried thyme or a single bay leaf extra
subs.notes: rosemary is distinctive here; thyme is the closest substitute

subs.name: toasted roti or thick papad
subs.notes: toasted roti torn and dunked in the soup makes a thoroughly satisfying meal

steps.text: Rinse the lentils under cold water until the water runs clear. If using regular masoor dal, no soaking is needed. Brown or green whole lentils benefit from 30 minutes soaking — they will cook more evenly.
Warm 3 tablespoons of olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add the onion, celery and carrot with a pinch of salt. Cook for 8 minutes until soft, fragrant and lightly golden.
Add the garlic and rosemary and stir for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the tomatoes and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they break down and the oil tints orange-red.

Add the rinsed lentils, bay leaves and all the stock. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to a steady simmer. Partially cover and cook for 25–30 minutes until the lentils are completely tender and beginning to fall apart at the edges.

Discard the bay leaves. For a creamier texture, use a ladle to scoop out one-third of the soup and blend until smooth, then stir it back in — this gives body without losing the whole lentils. Season generously with salt, pepper and a squeeze of lemon.
Ladle into deep bowls, drizzle generously with the remaining olive oil, crack over more black pepper and serve alongside a slice of toasted bread for dipping.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: masoor-dal → toor dal, moong dal or chana dal (toor dal gives a slightly softer, more soupy texture; all work well in this broth); celery → the green tops of spring onion or leek (spring onion tops give freshness; leek gives sweetness similar to celery); rosemary → dried thyme or a single bay leaf extra (rosemary is distinctive here; thyme is the closest substitute); bread → toasted roti or thick papad (toasted roti torn and dunked in the soup makes a thoroughly satisfying meal)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Use masoor dal (whole red lentils with skin, not the split orange variety) for the closest texture to Italian lentils. Toor dal also works but gives a slightly different flavour. Make this in a pressure cooker for speed: sauté the vegetables in the cooker base on medium flame, add tomatoes and cook 3 minutes, then add lentils and stock, seal and cook for 4 whistles on medium heat. Open after pressure releases, check and adjust consistency. The pressure-cooker method cuts cook time from 30 to 12 minutes.
